title: Test Rules for pytest + Python 3.13+
description: Machine-readable test rules for agents. ALL rules are mandatory.

TEST-001.name: Use HTTPStatus Constants
TEST-001.ifThen: IF asserting HTTP status code THEN use named constant from tests/constants.py

TEST-004.name: Use respx for HTTP Mocking
TEST-004.ifThen: IF mocking external HTTP calls THEN use @respx.mock + respx.get/post().mock()

TEST-005.name: Use pytest.approx for Floats
TEST-005.ifThen: IF comparing float values THEN use pytest.approx()

TEST-006.name: Specific Exception Types in pytest.raises
TEST-006.ifThen: IF testing exception THEN use pytest.raises(SpecificError, match='pattern')

TEST-007.name: Behavior-Driven Test Names
TEST-007.ifThen: IF naming test function THEN use test_<unit>_<behavior>_<condition> pattern

TEST-008.name: Mock Dependencies, Not Code Under Test
TEST-008.ifThen: IF isolating code under test THEN mock its dependencies, not itself

TEST-010.name: Every Test Must Have Assertions
TEST-010.ifThen: IF writing test THEN include at least one assert

PARAM-001.name: Use Parametrize for Multiple Inputs
PARAM-001.ifThen: IF testing same behavior with multiple inputs THEN use @pytest.mark.parametrize
